resposta:
solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def canBeEqual(self, target: List[int], arr: List[int]) -> bool:
        return sorted(target) == sorted(arr)
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n log n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution sorts both input arrays and compares them. This approach leverages the efficiency of sorting algorithms for determining if the arrays contain the same elements regardless of order.  Sorting has a time complexity of O(n log n), and the space complexity is also O(n) due to the creation of a new sorted list.
